MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION / Plaintiff Triio, Llcs Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication Against Defendant Vu Nguyen
Off Calendar Aug-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF TRIIO, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Defendant Vu Nguyen as follows: Plaintiff Triio, LLC's motion for summary adjudication against Vu Nguyen as to the second, sixth, and seventh causes of action in Triio, LLC's complaint is denied as to each cause of action. Triio, LLC has not met its summary judgment burden of entirely disposing of a cause of action. (CCP 437c(f)(1).) The second, sixth, and seventh causes of action all require a showing of damages. (CACI 325; Stanley v. Richmond (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 1070, 1086; Engalla v. Permanente Medical Group, Inc. (1997) 15 Cal.4th 951, 974.) Triio, LLC's request for judicial notice of its complaint is unopposed and granted. Triio, LLC's complaint requests general and special damages and punitive damages. Triio, LLC argues it has been deprived of certain business income and potential future business income. While Triio, LLC submitted evidence as to the prior business income it seeks, it provided no evidence as to the amount of damages for lost future business or punitive damages. (SUMF 6, 12, 18.) Therefore, Triio, LLC has not completely disposed of the second, sixth, and seventh causes of action and summary adjudication is denied as to each of those causes of action. The court declines to rule on the evidentiary objections submitted by Triio, LLC because they are not material to the court's decision. (CCP 437c(q).) Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order that complies with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Cross-Defendant Joshua Topolski'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to Cross-Defendant Joshua Topolski's Demurrer To 1st Amended Cross-Complaint, Defendant Joshua Topolski's demurrer to the fifth cause of action for violation of Stored Communications Act and the tenth cause of action for indemnification and declaratory relief is overruled. Cross-complainants Manuel Chavez and Vhantom are given leave to file the second amended cross-complaint attached to the opposition papers. The fifth cause of action sufficiently alleges that Triio and Topolski intentionally accessed a facility through which an electronic communications service is provided. Chavez and Vhantom's e-mail server is alleged to be such a facility. Chavez and Vhantom also allege that Vhantom was the provider and the access was not authorized. These allegations are accepted as true on this demurrer, notwithstanding the position of Triio and Topolski that documents attached to the complaint show otherwise. Chavez complied with the August 18, 2015 order on the prior demurrer by attaching the contract that is alleged to be the basis for the tenth cause of action. The tenth cause of action alleges a claim for contractual, not equitable, indemnity. While a motion is usually required to obtain leave to amend a pleading, no useful purpose would be achieved by requiring Chavez and Vhantom to file a motion for leave to file a second amended complaint. Because a party may plead alternative theories of liability, there is nothing improper about the new claim sought to be added by the second amended complaint. Moreover, there is a strong policy in favor of allowing amendments to pleadings and because no trial date has been set, there is no prejudice to Triio and Topolski by granting leave to file the second amended complaint. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL SCHREIBER OF THE BRACAMONTES & VLASAK, TEL. NO. 1-415-835-6777 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PAUL MICAHEL VUKSICK OF SF GENERAL COUNSEL, TEL. NO. 1-415-788-7045 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT TRIIO, LLC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT - Plaintiff's Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Cross Defendant's demurrer to the 1st cause of action is Overruled. 16 of the cross-complaint states "Topolski changed the wage structure to wages of $4,543.28 per month to Chavez and the balance as a commission for his services payable in the amount of $6,394.22 to Vhantom." Thus, P has adequately pled a modification. Further, 2 of the complaint alleges Chavez to be the sole member and 100% owner of Vhantom. Cross Defendant's demurrer to the 3rd cause of action is Overrulled. The complaint alleges Cross Defendant Topolski exercised total dominion and control over Triio and held such a unity of identity and interest that the acts of Triio complained of should be deemed to be his and the entity disregarded. (See Cross Complaint 4.) Therefore D's allegations are sufficient to plead alter ego theory. Cross Defendant's demurrer to the 4th cause of action is Overrulled. The Cross-Complaint alleges Triio has breached the promissory note. (See Cross Complaint 12.) Cross Defendant's demurrer to the 5th cause of action is Sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Unauthorized access to stored communications that have not been properly alleged in the 5th COA. Cross Defendant's demurrer to the 6th cause of action is Overrulled. The Cross Complaint alleges Defendant Chavez had a reasonable expectation of privacy in email communications, that was violated by Topolski, and as a result Defendant Chavez suffered harm. (See Cross Complaint 39 - 44.) Cross Defendant's demurrer to the 8th cause of action is Overruled. Corporations Code 17704.09(d) states: "A member shall discharge the duties to a limited liability company and the other members under this title or under the operating agreement and exercise any rights consistent with the obligat faith and fair dealing." Here the Cross-Complaint alleges Cross Defendant's breached their obligation of good faith and fair dealing as to Defendant Chavez. (See Cross Complaint 48 - 50.) Cross Defendant's demurrer to the 9th cause of action is Overruled. Civil Code 45 states "[l]ibel is a false and unprivileged publication by writing, printing, picture, effigy, or other fixed representation to the eye, which exposes any person to hatred, contempt, ridicule, or obloquy, or which causes him to be shunned or avoided, or which has a tendency to injure him in his occupation." The Cross Complaint alleges Topolski violated Civil Code 45 through a written statement declaring Defendant Chavez acted unethically in his business. Cross Defendant's demurrer to the 10th cause of action is Sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Cross Defendant makes reference to the provision for indemnification of members, however, this provision is not attached to the Cross Complaint, therefore the cross complaint is uncertain as to the 10th Cause of Action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
